problem_statement: Intermittent HTTP 502 errors from the API gateway. Approximately 40% of requests fail, while 60% succeed. No recent deployments.

app-logs.text: 502 Bad Gateway — upstream TLS handshake failed for backend-3:443
502 Bad Gateway — upstream TLS handshake failed for backend-5:443
502 Bad Gateway — upstream TLS handshake failed for backend-5:443

tls-certs.text: Rotation job cert-rotate-20260501 completed: 3 of 5 backends updated successfully. backend-3 FAILED (connection refused during deploy), backend-5 FAILED (deploy agent not running)

lb-health.text: Backend pool health: backend-1 UP, backend-2 UP, backend-3 DOWN (TLS error), backend-4 UP, backend-5 DOWN (TLS error)
Routing algorithm: round-robin across 5 backends. Health-check interval: 30s, threshold: 2 failures
Backend pool health: all 5 backends UP (last full-healthy: 2026-05-01T23:58:00Z)

network-logs.text: No blocked connections to backend subnet 10.0.5.0/24
All uplinks to backend rack operational — no CRC errors, no drops
api.example.com resolved correctly to load-balancer VIP — TTL 300s, no NXDOMAIN

deploy-history.text: Last deployment: api-service v2.14.3 rolled out 4 days ago — all backends verified healthy post-deploy
Scheduled cert rotation job cert-rotate-20260501 triggered at 02:00 UTC

expected_root_cause: The automated TLS certificate rotation job (cert-rotate-20260501) failed to update 2 of 5 backend servers (backend-3: connection refused, backend-5: deploy agent not running). The old certificates expired on 2026-05-01, causing TLS handshake failures for requests routed to those backends, producing intermittent 502 errors proportional to the 2/5 unhealthy ratio.
